result: If I could change the world in one way, I would make it impossible to inflict suffering on another conscious being without feeling it yourself. Not as a metaphor or a moral lesson—but as a *physical law of reality*, as immediate and inescapable as gravity. Every act of cruelty, neglect, or exploitation would resonate in the perpetrator’s own nerves, their own heart. Empathy wouldn’t be a choice; it would be a constraint woven into existence itself. The world that emerged from that single change would be unrecognizable—and, I think, worth everything.
